CIPHER.1384.cipherString: To become properly acquainted with a truth, we must first have disbelieved it, and disputed against it.
CIPHER.1384.question: Novalis offers an observation that has been encoded as an Aristocrat cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1384.author: Novalis

CIPHER.1385.cipherString: Making a book is a craft, as is making a clock; it takes more than wit to become an author.
CIPHER.1385.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Jean de la Bruyére, written as an Atbash Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1385.author: Jean de la Bruyére

CIPHER.1386.cipherString: What is more mortifying than to feel that you have missed the plum for lack of courage to shake the tree?
CIPHER.1386.question: A vending machine just dispensed a candy wrapper with this Aristocrat code by Logan P. Smith. What does it say?
CIPHER.1386.author: Logan P. Smith

CIPHER.1387.cipherString: The day I stop enjoying football is the day I'll go to drink tea with my mom.
CIPHER.1387.question: You open a forgotten email draft saved in the school's computer system. It holds a quote by Juan Román Riquelme encoded in a Porta using a keyword of LATIN. What does it say?
CIPHER.1387.author: Juan Román Riquelme

CIPHER.1388.cipherString: We shall describe the ordinary phenomena of tides.
CIPHER.1388.question: Astronomers notice this repeating sequence in deep-space data. It's actually a quote encoded with the Nihilist Substitution cipher with a keyword of MOON and polybius key of EARTHSHINE. What does it say?
CIPHER.1388.author: Theory of tides

CIPHER.1389.cipherString: The rest of the universe was disappointing at first glance.
CIPHER.1389.question: You intercepted this transmission from a rogue weather balloon. Decode the Baconian version of Lois McMaster Bujold's famous words. What does it say?
CIPHER.1389.author: Lois McMaster Bujold

CIPHER.1390.cipherString: How do we know the earth isn't flat? If it were flat, cats would have already pushed everything off of it.
CIPHER.1390.question: While analyzing the background noise from a physics experiment, you detect this pattern. It's a quote, encoded using a Caesar. What does it say?
CIPHER.1390.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1391.cipherString: To have ideas is to gather flowers; to think is to weave them into garlands.
CIPHER.1391.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Nihilist Substitution encoded quote by Anne Sophie Swetchine with a keyword length of 6. The 31st through 44th cipher units (89 75 33 36 49 34 88 87 23 74 38 27 96 65) decode to be THINKISTOWEAVE. What does it say?
CIPHER.1391.author: Anne Sophie Swetchine

CIPHER.1392.cipherString: My mother is a school teacher and my father is an electrician.
CIPHER.1392.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Fractionated Morse encoded quote by Jai Courtney. You are told that the quote has AND MY in it corresponding to the encoded text S V V C L G P, meaning C = ●××; G = –●–; L = ––×; P = –××; S = ×●–; V = ×–●. What does it say?
CIPHER.1392.author: Jai Courtney

CIPHER.1393.cipherString: Life is painting a picture, not doing a sum.
CIPHER.1393.question: You find a coded sequence scratched into the surface of a lab stool. It turns out to be a quote by Oliver Wendell Holmes, written in a Baconian. You are told that the cipher text IS THE YUMMY ENEMY AGREE decodes to be PAIN. What does it say?
CIPHER.1393.author: Oliver Wendell Holmes

CIPHER.1394.cipherString: ¡Por fin se han casado mis amigos! Hubo doscientos cincuenta invitados y se casaron en una bonita iglesia.
CIPHER.1394.question: This passage in Spanish is a retelling of a special event. It has been encoded as a K1 Xenocrypt with an English keyword. What does it say?

CIPHER.1395.cipherString: A well-spent day brings happy sleep.
CIPHER.1395.question: An old robot prototype suddenly prints this encoded text. It's a quote by Leonardo da Vinci, encrypted using an Affine with a=11 and b=9. What does it say?
CIPHER.1395.author: Leonardo da Vinci

CIPHER.1396.cipherString: So comes snow after fire, and even dragons have their endings.
CIPHER.1396.question: Written in glow-in-the-dark ink on the ceiling of the bio lab, uncover this Baconian quote by J.R.R. Tolkien. What does it say?
CIPHER.1396.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

CIPHER.1397.cipherString: Of all man's work of art, a cathedral is greatest. A vast and majestic tree is greater than that.
CIPHER.1397.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Ward Beecher, encoded using a Checkerboard Cipher with a polybius key of REDISCOVERER. What does it say?
CIPHER.1397.author: Ward Beecher

CIPHER.1398.cipherString: My whole life, I had been taught to read and study, to seek understanding in knowledge of history, of cultures.
CIPHER.1398.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Aristocrat cipher text by Meghan O'Rourke. What does it say?
CIPHER.1398.author: Meghan O'Rourke

CIPHER.1399.question: Your lab partner discovered this pattern on the back of a graphing calculator. It's a quote, encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 10354195 91892823 decode to?

CIPHER.1400.cipherString: In the truest sense, freedom cannot be bestowed; it must be achieved.
CIPHER.1400.question: President Franklin D. Roosevelt was often heard to say the following phrase which has been encoded as an Atbash. What does it say?
CIPHER.1400.author: President Franklin D. Roosevelt

CIPHER.1401.cipherString: Sometimes I wish life had subtitles.
CIPHER.1401.question: Solve this quote that has been encoded using the Affine Cipher. You are told that the cipher text NC decodes to be HA. What does it say?
CIPHER.1401.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1402.cipherString: There are certain moments in the history of a nation when the choices made define the decades to come.
CIPHER.1402.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Georgios A. Papandreou encoded as a Caesar cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1402.author: Georgios A. Papandreou

CIPHER.1403.cipherString: Este año quiero viajar a España para hacer las prácticas en un hospital y necesito mejorar mi nivel de español.
CIPHER.1403.question: Below is some Spanish-language text about someone's plans that has been encoded as a K1 Xenocrypt with an English keyword.

CIPHER.1404.question: A phrase has been encoded using the Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 90371 20334585 decode to?

CIPHER.1405.cipherString: Are you reading books and learning something new each day? Tiny battles like these are the ones that will define your future self.
CIPHER.1405.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Patristocrat message onto the screen—decode this James Clear advice using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains TINY B corresponding with the ciphertext QKYU F on the second line. What does it say?
CIPHER.1405.author: James Clear

CIPHER.1406.cipherString: Time is funny. It seems to move too slowly through the worst times and too quickly through the best.
CIPHER.1406.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote by Melanie A. Smith written in a Complete Columnar cipher. The quote has THROUGH somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1406.author: Melanie A. Smith

CIPHER.1407.cipherString: There were clouds like sharks with open jaws in the sky that morning.
CIPHER.1407.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote by Tanith Lee, encrypted as a Porta. The 40th through 45th cipher characters (EPQHXC) decode to be THESKY. What does it say?
CIPHER.1407.author: Tanith Lee

CIPHER.1408.cipherString: Every element, whether mineral or organic, that is required for nutrition is found in the vegetable kingdom.
CIPHER.1408.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Dr. Edward Smith encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.1408.author: Dr. Edward Smith

CIPHER.1409.cipherString: Poetry lifts the veil from the hidden beauty of the world, and makes familiar objects be as if they were not familiar.
CIPHER.1409.question: Someone left this encrypted quote about writing scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's by Percy Bysshe Shelley, written as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word HIDDEN on the first line. What does it say?
CIPHER.1409.author: Percy Bysshe Shelley

CIPHER.1410.cipherString: Save your rejections so that later when you are famous you can show them to people and laugh.
CIPHER.1410.question: The following quote by Meg Cabot needs to be decoded with the Checkerboard Cipher. The deciphered text starts with SAVEYOUR. What does it say?
CIPHER.1410.author: Meg Cabot

CIPHER.1411.cipherString: Lots of things can happen when you collect more stars! So keep practicing, and keep playing those games!
CIPHER.1411.question: Hidden in the source code of a retro video game, this Aristocrat puzzle reveals a quote by Super Mario Brothers. What does it say?
CIPHER.1411.author: Super Mario Brothers

CIPHER.1412.cipherString: It's only a paper moon, Sailing over a cardboard sea.
CIPHER.1412.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by E. Y. ('Yip') Harburg encoded as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote has PAPER M in it corresponding to the encoded text N A M H U E Z O, meaning A = ●×●; E = ●–●; H = ––●; M = –×●; N = ●––; O = –×–; U = ×●×; Z = ××–. What does it say?
CIPHER.1412.author: E. Y. ('Yip') Harburg

CIPHER.1413.cipherString: Space isn't remote at all. It's only an hour's drive away if your car could go straight upwards.
CIPHER.1413.question: A mysterious app update added this Complete Columnar lock screen—it's actually Fred Hoyle's quote. The quote has AN HOUR'S somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1413.author: Fred Hoyle
